pi_items.source: Found exactly in setup_task.sh where the task data is generated.
Found in export_result.sh and verifier.py as the expected path.
Found in export_result.sh and verifier.py as the expected path.
Confirmed as the maximum distance limit for propagation sync in verifier.py. Python orbit simulation yields a true max distance of ~99.8 km, safely within this 150.0 km upper bound.
Confirmed as the minimum distance limit for collision avoidance in verifier.py. Python orbit simulation yields a true min distance of ~33.7 km, safely exceeding this 15.0 km lower bound.
Confirmed as the minimum row requirement in verifier.py. 14 days of data at 3600-second steps yields 337 points, making 330 a correct minimum threshold.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic colocation directive via setup_task.sh. All expected file paths (/home/ga/Desktop/colocation_directive.txt, /home/ga/GMAT_output/geo_colocation.script, /home/ga/GMAT_output/colocation_ephem.txt) match the scripts and task description. The maximum distance limit (150.0 km), minimum distance limit (15.0 km), and minimum row threshold (330) are correctly verified against the verifier.py logic. An independent python orbit simulation confirmed these thresholds safely bound the actual distance variation (~33.7 km to ~99.8 km) and row count (337 points) of the two satellites over the 14 day period.
